summary: A medieval-style fortified structure with a distinctive cross-shaped floor plan. Features two tall towers with crenellated tops connected by a central keep. The building uses alternating layers of wood and stonebrick for walls, with a dark wood cross-shaped interior courtyard. Surrounded by a stonebrick perimeter wall and defensive moat.

shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along north-south and east-west axes

rebuild_hints: Build two tall towers (approximately 12 blocks high) at diagonal corners
Connect towers with horizontal stonebrick beams at top
Create cross-shaped interior courtyard using dark wood blocks
Alternate horizontal layers of wood and stonebrick for wall texture (approximately 2-3 block bands)
Add crenellations (1-block high stonebrick pattern) along top edges
Construct outer perimeter wall using stonebrick blocks in a square pattern
Add window openings at regular intervals on tower faces
Surround base with moat/ditch using dirt blocks
Place small square structures on east and west sides of courtyard
Ensure bilateral symmetry across both axes
uncertainties: Exact interior room layout and floor divisions not clearly visible
Precise window placement and size varies by angle
Depth of moat and exact perimeter wall thickness uncertain
Whether interior courtyard is fully enclosed or open to sky
Exact block count for alternating wood/stonebrick layers
Small structures on east/west sides may be decorative or functional
